title: Adjust timeline end date based on date column of actual end date if sooner
excerpt:
I have a timeline column and a actual completion date column. When the actual completion date column is populated, if the date is before the timeline end date, i want the timeline to reflect the new end date. 
Ie. Planne…

posts.author: Calgat (Clare Taggart)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a timeline column and a actual completion date column. When the actual completion date column is populated, if the date is before the timeline end date, i want the timeline to reflect the new end date.

Ie. Planned project timeline end date was 31st March. Actual date was 20th March. so timeline needs to update to show that sprint ended on the 20th March

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Calgat This can be done using one of the formula apps in the marketplace or the no-code tool Make (or similar).

Jim - The Monday Man
What is Make & How can it help you with monday?
Schedule a free initial consultation here

posts.author: Matt_Frost (Dr Matt Frost - www.frostgroup.uk)
posts.cookedHtml:
I don’t think theres a way to do it that way around without using something like Zapier or Make. But you can use the new AI feature on the date column if you update the end date on the timeline.
You use the prompt “Same as end” it will update if the date column when the timeline end updates.

posts.author: Liz-Perry (Liz Perry - Polished Geek)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Calgat

You can do this with monday’s Workflow Builder if you have an Enterprise monday.com account.

Set up the following Workflow automation:

Screenshot 2025-02-04 at 10.39.30 AM1436×786 113 KB

This will set you up so that “When status changes to Done, update timeline end date to be today’s day”.

I hope that helps your use case!

Liz Perry
Sr Process Consultant at Polished Geek

posts.author: Bree (Bree)
posts.cookedHtml:
I think you’re onto something!

However, it appears that this method will return today’s date. Is there a way to have the timeline’s end match the date in the date column?

posts.author: Liz-Perry (Liz Perry - Polished Geek)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Bree,

There’s a new automation center that is currently in Beta that allows this. (https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/31585338491922-New-Automation-Builder)

Screenshot 2026-03-20 at 9.09.39 AM1000×617 37.2 KB

It will allow you to change the timeline start or end date to be dynamically set to the date column upon trigger.

I use both of these methods often (method 1 in conjunction with the normal monday dependency automation) with clients.

I’d be interested to know more about your use case so I can help!

Kind regards,

Liz Perry
Sr Process Consultant at Polished Geek
